title: Introduction to transclust
text: transclust package comes sample dataset can used demonstration purposes. dataset contains epidemiological information set isolates. load dataset, can use following command: first read sequence file prepared using procedure described Hawken et al. (2022). can read sequence file using ape’s read.dna function: Now sequence file loaded, can extract variable positions alignment. var_pos variable contains logical vector indicating columns alignment variable. dna_pt_labels variable contains labels sequences alignment, facility_trace matrix containing trace data isolates. valid set labels determined checking labels dna_pt_labels variable present facility_trace matrix. can subset alignment include variable positions isolates valid labels: use helper function get_snp_dist_matrix() calculate SNP distance matrix isolates: snp_dist variable contains SNP distance matrix, square matrix entry represents number SNP differences two isolates. diagonal entries zero, represent distance isolate .
code: load(system.file("extdata", "example.RData", package = "transclust")) dna_aln <- read.dna(system.file("extdata", "example.fasta", package = "transclust"), format = "fasta") # Get all variable positions in the alignment var_pos <- apply(dna_aln, 2, function(x) sum(x == x[1]) < nrow(dna_aln)) # Only keep those labels that are in the trace matrix valid_labels <- dna_pt_labels[labels(dna_aln)] %in% row.names(facility_trace) dna_var <- dna_aln[valid_labels, var_pos] snp_dist <- get_snp_dist_matrix(dna_var)

what: Clustering isolates using a hard SNP threshold
title: Introduction to transclust
text: Now ’ve loaded data, can start clustering isolates. First, want use hard SNP threshold cluster isolates. get_tn_clusters_snp_thresh() function takes SNP distance matrix threshold value input returns list clusters. use threshold 10 SNPs example: can visualize clusters phylogenetic tree using plot_clusters_phylo() function. , first need generate phylogenetic tree. can using get_phylo_tree() function. use maximum parsimony method example:  isn’t SNP threshold approach. method tries preserve clusters within phylogenetic tree structure, can also cluster isolates just based SNP distance matrix regard
code: clusters_snp <- get_tn_clusters_snp_thresh(snp_dist, 10) # Generate a parsimony tree phylo_tree <- get_phylo_tree(dna_var, snp_dist, "pars") plot_clusters_phylo(phylo_tree, clusters_snp) #> Warning in max(tree$edge.length): no non-missing arguments to max; returning #> -Inf

what: Clustering isolates using a threshold-free approach
title: Introduction to transclust
text: addition hard SNP threshold approach, can also use threshold-free approach cluster isolates. package implements method described Hawken et al. (2022) get_tn_clusters_sv_index() function. method uses threshold-free approach identify clusters based structure phylogenetic tree. use parsimony tree example. function takes inputs SNP threshold method: clusters_sv object contains clusters identified using threshold-free approach. plot shows phylogenetic tree clusters highlighted.
code: clusters_sv <- get_tn_clusters_sv_index(     dna_var, snp_dist, adm_seqs, adm_pos_pt_seqs,     dna_pt_labels, dates, phylo_tree ) plot_clusters_phylo(phylo_tree, clusters_sv) #> Warning in max(tree$edge.length): no non-missing arguments to max; returning #> -Inf

title: Introduction to transclust
text: two trees look quite different. can compare clusters generated using two methods using compare_clusters() function. function generates heatmap showing overlap clusters indicating proportion isolates cluster also present clusters generated method. can use function, need remove singleton clusters, clusters contain one isolate – useful comparison:
code: # remove singleton clusters from both clustering methods clusters_snp <- remove_singleton_clusters(clusters_snp) clusters_sv <- remove_singleton_clusters(clusters_sv) # compare the clusters compare_clusters(clusters_snp, clusters_sv) +     # change the color palette     scale_fill_paletteer_c("grDevices::Mint", direction = -1) +     # add a title     ggtitle("Comparison of clusters using SNP threshold and threshold-free methods") +     # add x and y axis labels     xlab("Threshold-free clusters") +     ylab("SNP threshold clusters") +     # center the title and rotate x axis labels     theme(         plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),         axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)     ) #> → heatmap built with `geom_tile()`

what: Some statistical analysis of the clustering results
title: Introduction to transclust
text: transclust package also provides functions statistical analysis clustering results. example, can use intra_cluster_genetic_var_analysis() function calculate genetic variation within cluster: dataframe columns total number variable sites, also proportions six types base substitutions present cluster. isn’t intuitive just look , can plot . First, drop possible NA values. plot, want compare relative frequencies different types base substitutions, also exclude first column - total number variable sites. subset data frame include first rows clarity plot: can plot proportions type base substitution cluster. , ggplot2 package used create stacked bar plot:  can clearly see specific mutational signatures emerge clusters compared population whole.
code: mut_var_df <- intra_cluster_genetic_var_analysis(clusters_sv, dna_aln) mut_var_df <- head(na.omit(mut_var_df)[, -1]) # Add column for cluster using rownames before pivoting mut_var_df$cluster <- row.names(mut_var_df)  # Convert the data frame to long format for ggplot mut_var_df_long <- pivot_longer(     mut_var_df,     cols = -cluster,     names_to = "mutation_type",     values_to = "proportion" )  # Create the stacked bar plot ggplot(mut_var_df_long, aes(x = cluster, y = proportion, fill = mutation_type)) +     geom_bar(stat = "identity") +     labs(x = "Cluster", y = "Proportion", fill = "Mutation Type") +     scale_fill_paletteer_d("ggsci::lanonc_lancet")

Rows days, columns patients. floor_mat: matrix tracking patient locations floor hospital. Rows days, columns patients. room_mat: matrix tracking room assignments patients. Rows days, columns patients. dates: vector admission dates patient. dna_pt_labels: vector mapping sequence IDs isolates patient IDs. ip_seqs: vector sequence IDs correspond intake patient sequences presumed imported. ip_seqs_3days: vector sequence IDs correspond intake patient sequences tested positive within 3 days admission.

what: Adjusted Mutual Information from Contingency Table — ami_from_contingency
title: Adjusted Mutual Information from Contingency Table — ami_from_contingency
text: Computes Adjusted Mutual Information (AMI) contingency table. AMI measures mutual information two clusterings, adjusted chance. Values range 0 1, 1 indicates perfect agreement 0 indicates independence.

title: Adjusted Mutual Information from Contingency Table — ami_from_contingency
Adjusted Mutual Information from Contingency Table — ami_from_contingency
text: cont_table contingency table (matrix) rows correspond clusters first assignment columns clusters second.

title: Adjusted Mutual Information from Contingency Table — ami_from_contingency
text: numeric value representing Adjusted Mutual Information.

title: Adjusted Mutual Information from Contingency Table — ami_from_contingency
text: Adjusted Mutual Information calculated : $$AMI = \frac{MI - E[MI]}{\max(H(U), H(V)) - E[MI]}$$ \(MI\) mutual information, \(E[MI]\) expected mutual information random permutation, \(H(U)\) \(H(V)\) entropies two clusterings.

title: Adjusted Mutual Information from Contingency Table — ami_from_contingency
text: Vinh, N. X., Epps, J., Bailey, J. (2010). Information theoretic measures clusterings comparison: Variants, properties, normalization correction chance. Journal Machine Learning Research, 11, 2837-2854.

what: Adjusted Rand Index from Contingency Table — ari_from_contingency
title: Adjusted Rand Index from Contingency Table — ari_from_contingency
text: Computes Adjusted Rand Index (ARI) contingency table. ARI measures similarity two clusterings, adjusted chance. Values range -1 1, 1 indicates perfect agreement, 0 indicates random agreement, negative values indicate less agreement expected chance.

title: Adjusted Rand Index from Contingency Table — ari_from_contingency
Adjusted Rand Index from Contingency Table — ari_from_contingency
text: cont_table contingency table (matrix) rows correspond clusters first assignment columns clusters second.

title: Adjusted Rand Index from Contingency Table — ari_from_contingency
text: numeric value representing Adjusted Rand Index.

title: Adjusted Rand Index from Contingency Table — ari_from_contingency
text: Adjusted Rand Index calculated : $$ARI = \frac{Index - Expected}{MaxIndex - Expected}$$ Index sum \(\binom{n_{ij}}{2}\) cells, Expected derived row column marginals, MaxIndex average row column sum contributions. values \(n_{ij}\) contingency table, \(a_i\) row sums, \(b_j\) column sums, \(n\) total number isolates.

title: Adjusted Rand Index from Contingency Table — ari_from_contingency
text: Hubert, L. Arabie, P. (1985). Comparing partitions. Journal Classification, 2(1), 193-218.

title: Categorize cluster overlap — categorize_cluster_overlap
text: function categorizes overlap isolates cluster, based earliest collected isolate patients deemed cluster (hereafter referred "index isolate"), overlap explanations isolates cluster. categories : "patient--patient": index isolate admission-positive overlap explanation converts cluster. "weak-index": index isolate admission-positive first surveillance patient admission overlap explanation isolates cluster. "missing-intermediate": index isolate admission-positive least one convert cluster overlap explanation. "false-negative-index": index isolate admission-positive overlap explanation converts cluster barring one (deemed false negative index). "missing-source": index isolate admission-positive overlap explanation one convert cluster. "multiply-colonized-index": index isolate cluster admission-positive, "multiply-colonized index" overlap explanation isolates cluster. "multiply-colonized-index-missing-intermediate": index isolate cluster admission-positive, least one convert cluster overlap explanation. "inexplicable": catch-category cases fit categories. Currently, index overlap explanations enough explain clusters.
